approval.allowance.name: approval.allowance
approval.allowance.description: Inspect a token allowance: surfaces raw value, unlimited-allowance flag, and coverage of a requested amount.

allowance.description: Current allowance (uint256 string). Defaults to 0 when omitted.

requested_amount.description: Amount being requested by the transaction (uint256 string).

approval.cover_inputs.name: approval.cover_inputs
approval.cover_inputs.description: Whether the token allowance covers the requested input amount.

allowances_cover_inputs.description: Caller-supplied override; takes precedence when present.

requested_amount.description: Amount being requested by the transaction.

clock.now.name: clock.now
clock.now.description: Current Unix timestamp (seconds) from the daemon's clock.
clock.now.stateDependency: none (host wall clock, not wallet state)

oracle.effective_rate_bps.name: oracle.effective_rate_bps
oracle.effective_rate_bps.description: Effective rate of a swap vs the oracle's mid-market price, expressed in basis points (negative = unfavourable).
oracle.effective_rate_bps.stateDependency: external price feed (oracle mid-market); no wallet-state read

oracle.usd_value.name: oracle.usd_value
oracle.usd_value.description: Convert a token amount to its USD valuation via a price oracle.
oracle.usd_value.stateDependency: external price feed (coingecko/chainlink); under ADR-009 this resolves to the DIRECT read of token_holdings.price_value (LiveField) instead of a live oracle call

source.description: Which price source to query. `coingecko` is HTTP-based and works out of the box; `chainlink` reads on-chain feeds and needs POLICY_RPC_CHAIN_RPCS configured per chain.

portfolio.balance.name: portfolio.balance
portfolio.balance.description: Wallet balance of a specific token.

portfolio.input_fraction_bps.name: portfolio.input_fraction_bps
portfolio.input_fraction_bps.description: Fraction of the wallet's portfolio (denominated in the input asset) the transaction is spending, in basis points.

scopeball.evaluate_v3.name: scopeball.evaluate_v3
scopeball.evaluate_v3.description: Phase 5D mock — echo (envelopes, eval_context, wallet_id) back as policyRequest.actions with empty state_before / deltas / state_after. Real implementation arrives in Phase 6 (reducer + state sync).
scopeball.evaluate_v3.stateDependency: whole-wallet state (holdings/approvals/positions) folded through the reducer to produce State₂ (mock today; Phase 6 wires the real reducer)

wallet_id.description: WalletId (address + chains). Carried verbatim by the mock; Phase 6 will key state persistence off this.

envelopes.description: List of caller-built Action envelopes (typed PDF FSM ActionBody). The mock echoes these into policyRequest.actions.

stat_window.snapshot.name: stat_window.snapshot
stat_window.snapshot.description: Rolling-window snapshot of recent on-chain activity for the wallet.
stat_window.snapshot.stateDependency: cross-request activity history ([H]); not in the state DB today (only state_deltas is partial)

stat_window.swap_stats.name: stat_window.swap_stats
stat_window.swap_stats.description: Per-action stats over a sliding 24h window (volume + count).
stat_window.swap_stats.stateDependency: cross-request swap history over a 24h window ([H]); not in the state DB today

action.description: Action keyword (e.g. "swap").

external.morpho_market_recognized.name: external.morpho_market_recognized
external.morpho_market_recognized.description: MORPHO: resolve a Morpho Blue market_id (carried by the venue) to its (loanToken, collateralToken, oracle, irm, lltv) and report whether the oracle + collateral + market are on Morpho's recognized allowlist (the RED-warning list). Permissionless market creation is unique to Morpho Blue. Drives morpho-blue-unrecognized-market-supply-warn. fail-open.
external.morpho_market_recognized.stateDependency: external Morpho market registry / recognized-market allowlist (not state DB)

venue.description: LendingVenue::MorphoBlue carrying the market_id hex; resolved to market params.

external.morpho_vault_recognized.name: external.morpho_vault_recognized
external.morpho_vault_recognized.description: MORPHO: given a MetaMorpho / Morpho vault (carried by the venue), report whether it is on a recognized-curator allowlist. Curator-routed bad debt (Stream/xUSD, Nov-2025) is unique to Morpho's curated-vault layer. Drives morpho-vault-unrecognized-deposit-warn. fail-open.
external.morpho_vault_recognized.stateDependency: external Morpho vault/curator registry (not state DB)

external.morpho_spender_is_bundler_core.name: external.morpho_spender_is_bundler_core
external.morpho_spender_is_bundler_core.description: MORPHO: is the ERC20 approve spender the Morpho Bundler3 CORE entrypoint (no access control) rather than a known access-controlled adapter? Registry-pure classification. Drives morpho-approve-to-bundler-core-warn (Apr-2025 Bundler3 approval front-run, ~$2.6M at risk). fail-open.
external.morpho_spender_is_bundler_core.stateDependency: Morpho address-book registry (bundler3 core vs adapters)

approval.unlimited_over_balance.name: approval.unlimited_over_balance
approval.unlimited_over_balance._structural_flag: REAL/implemented in crates/simulation/server/src/facts.rs (sim-server fact host), NOT the policy-rpc daemon. It sits in `planned` only because `methods` is byte-coupled to the daemon (catalog.test.ts drift test). Under ADR-009 (policy-rpc retiring, sim-server = fact host) the structural home for sim-server methods is an open question — see catalog_conformance.rs Task-5 flag.
approval.unlimited_over_balance.description: GEN-01: is the ERC20 approval to `spender` on `token` unlimited, and how many times the wallet's available balance does the approved amount cover? Emits TWO context fields from one call.

external.address_blocklisted.name: external.address_blocklisted
external.address_blocklisted.description: GEN-11: is `address` on an external reputation blocklist? Read from an external reputation feed, not the wallet-state DB.

list.description: Optional sanctions/blocklist identifier (e.g. ofac_sdn, eu_sanctions, jp_fefta) selecting WHICH list to screen against.

valuation.asset_usd.name: valuation.asset_usd
valuation.asset_usd.description: USD valuation of a single asset position: token amount × DB price. Replaces the live oracle.usd_value call with a DIRECT read of the refrigerated LiveField (ADR-009).

position.health_factor_after.name: position.health_factor_after
position.health_factor_after.description: Health factor of a lending position AFTER virtually applying the proposed borrow/withdraw (State₂). Reducer recomputes from refrigerated collaterals/debts/liquidation_threshold + price. Drives AAVE-01 (T3).

action.description: The proposed borrow/withdraw — applied via the reducer to produce State₂ before recomputing HF. Without it the method cannot compute the AFTER value.

perp.notional_to_collateral.name: perp.notional_to_collateral
perp.notional_to_collateral.description: Ratio of the proposed order's notional (size × markPrice) to the account's total collateral USD. SELF-VERIFY: on OpenPosition both inputs live in the action's live_inputs (size, markPrice, userAccountState.totalCollateralUsd) → this is a DERIVED read, not a whole-wallet fold. A cross-position collateral fold is a separate variant. Drives PERP-03.

action.description: The proposed perp order: size × markPrice = notional; userAccountState = collateral denominator.

portfolio.group_pct.name: portfolio.group_pct
portfolio.group_pct.description: Whole-wallet fold: percentage of total portfolio USD held in a token/category group AFTER the proposed action (State₂). PortfolioAggregate — gated on ADR-010 (T2). Output flattens per-group scalars into context fields.

group_axis.description: How holdings are grouped to form the share's denominator/numerator.

asset.description: The group key being measured (e.g. the acquired token for group_axis=token).

basis.description: state1 = current holdings; state2 = after the action is applied via the reducer.

valuation.tx_total_usd.name: valuation.tx_total_usd
valuation.tx_total_usd.description: Total USD outflow of the whole transaction (incl. multicall children): sum of net negative balance deltas × price_value. State₂ via reducer. Drives GEN-05.

action.description: Tx/multicall applied via reducer to sum net outflow.

valuation.gas_cost_usd.name: valuation.gas_cost_usd
valuation.gas_cost_usd.description: Gas cost in USD = gasEstimate (action field) × native token price_value. Drives GEN-07/08.

approval.set_for_all_state.name: approval.set_for_all_state
approval.set_for_all_state.description: Whether (collection, operator) already holds a set-for-all grant in wallet state. Boost for GEN-02 (the core allowlist check is a Cedar set literal).

perp.position_state.name: perp.position_state
perp.position_state.description: EXISTING perp position's side / effective leverage / unrealized-pnl % from wallet state (NOT carried in the action's live_inputs). Drives PERP-08 (crosses zero), PERP-09 (adding to a loser), PERP-11 (leverage up).

market.description: Perp market identifying the existing position.

perp.liq_distance_bp.name: perp.liq_distance_bp
perp.liq_distance_bp.description: Distance (bp) from the liquidation price to the mark price AFTER opening the position (State₂). Reducer computes the resulting liq_price. Drives PERP-02.

claim.source_held_state.name: claim.source_held_state
claim.source_held_state.description: Whether the airdrop claim's source contract matches a known-eligible claim in wallet state, and whether the claimed token is ALREADY held (gasless-permit-on-held-token scam). Drives AIR-02/03.

action.description: Claim action: source contract + claimed token.

launchpad.sale_match.name: launchpad.sale_match
launchpad.sale_match.description: Whether the claim/commit target matches the original sale contract recorded in wallet state (LaunchpadAllocation.sale_id). Drives LP-01.

token.peg_ratio.name: token.peg_ratio
token.peg_ratio.description: Ratio of an LST/stable token's market price_value to its declared peg (TokenKind.peg_to). < 1 signals depeg. Drives STK-01, UNI-13.

position.health_factor_with_volatility.name: position.health_factor_with_volatility
position.health_factor_with_volatility.description: AAVE-02: post-trade health factor (State₂, same reducer recompute as position.health_factor_after) PLUS the collateral-volatility class of the position's dominant collateral. Emits TWO context fields from one call so a single policy can gate on 'HF in the warn band AND collateral is volatile'. The volatility class is an ExternalInfo tag (stable/volatile) joined onto the refrigerated collateral set.

action.description: The proposed borrow/withdraw — applied via the reducer to produce State₂ before recomputing HF.

position.ltv_after.name: position.ltv_after
position.ltv_after.description: AAVE-06: loan-to-value of a lending position AFTER virtually applying the proposed action (State₂), expressed as a decimal ratio (debt_usd / collateral_usd). Reducer recompute_ltv from refrigerated collaterals/debts + price. For eMode-entry policies this is the effective leverage the new category LTV cap is checked against; the policy reads the category cap from the action's base context (categoryConfig.ltvBp).

action.description: The proposed eMode/borrow action — applied via the reducer to produce State₂ before recomputing LTV.

position.borrow_fraction_bps.name: position.borrow_fraction_bps
position.borrow_fraction_bps.description: AAVE-07: fraction (basis points) of the user's remaining borrow capacity this borrow consumes = borrow_usd / availableBorrowUsd. The base Borrow context carries availableBorrowUsd only as a U256 hex string and amountUsd only as an optional decimal, so the ratio cannot be formed by a pure Cedar action-field read — it needs a derived read that prices the borrow amount and divides by the precomputed UserLendingState.available_borrow_usd.

action.description: The proposed borrow: amount × price = numerator; userStateBefore.availableBorrowUsd = denominator.

reserve.is_siloed.name: reserve.is_siloed
reserve.is_siloed.description: AAVE-10: whether the borrow target reserve is configured as siloed (single-asset borrowing). The shipped ReserveState in the lending action context carries utilization / caps / frozen / paused flags but NOT the siloed flag, so this reserve-metadata bit must come from a dedicated read of the reserve config (reserve_state metadata), not a pure action-field.
stateDependency: positions(lending) reserve config (siloed flag); reserve metadata not surfaced as a base context field

reserve.isolation_debt_headroom_bps.name: reserve.isolation_debt_headroom_bps
reserve.isolation_debt_headroom_bps.description: AAVE-11: how close (basis points of the debt ceiling) the isolation-mode total debt would be AFTER this borrow = (isolationModeTotalDebt + borrow_usd) / debtCeiling. The debt ceiling and the protocol-global isolationModeTotalDebt are NOT base context fields — they are reserve/protocol-global metadata — so this proximity must be derived from those externals plus the proposed borrow.

venue.description: Lending venue identifying the isolated reserve.

action.description: The proposed borrow whose USD value is added to isolationModeTotalDebt.

reserve.cap_used_bp.name: reserve.cap_used_bp
reserve.cap_used_bp.description: AAVE-12: how full the reserve's supply/borrow cap would be AFTER this action, in basis points = (total + action.amount) / cap. ReserveState surfaces totalSupply/totalBorrow/supplyCap/borrowCap only as U256 hex strings (optional caps), which Cedar cannot compare numerically, so the used-bp ratio must be derived rather than read as an action-field. `cap_kind` selects supply vs borrow.

cap_kind.description: Which cap to measure against (supply for supply actions, borrow for borrow actions).

action.description: The proposed supply/borrow whose amount is added to the current total.

external.lst_price_divergence_bps.name: external.lst_price_divergence_bps
external.lst_price_divergence_bps.description: AAVE-09: divergence (basis points) between the Aave oracle's price for an LST/LRT collateral and its true market/backing price, read from an external feed. Pairs with token.peg_ratio (depeg) and the action's eMode category to gate high-LTV LST looping. External — not a wallet-state read.
external.lst_price_divergence_bps.stateDependency: external LST oracle-vs-market divergence feed (not state DB)

asset.description: The LST/LRT collateral asset whose oracle/market divergence is measured.

external.repay_swap_slippage_bps.name: external.repay_swap_slippage_bps
external.repay_swap_slippage_bps.description: AAVE-13: realized slippage (basis points, negative = unfavourable) of the embedded swap in a repay-with-collateral / collateral-swap, comparing the calldata's minAmountOut floor against the oracle fair price. The Repay action context carries no swap-floor field, so the floor and the fair price are both read externally (oracle mid-market + decoded calldata).
external.repay_swap_slippage_bps.stateDependency: external oracle mid-market price + decoded repay-with-collateral swap floor (not state DB)

action.description: The repay-with-collateral action whose embedded swap floor is compared to fair value.

external.oracle_freshness.name: external.oracle_freshness
external.oracle_freshness.description: AAVE-14: freshness of the borrow asset's price oracle — whether it is stale (ttl/confidence exceeded or L2 sequencer grace) and its age in seconds. Derived from the price LiveField's synced_at/ttl plus the L2 sequencer-uptime sentinel; surfaced as an external freshness signal rather than a base context field.
external.oracle_freshness.stateDependency: price LiveField synced_at/ttl/confidence + L2 sequencer-uptime sentinel (external freshness signal)

perp.order_leverage.name: perp.order_leverage
perp.order_leverage.description: PERP-01: the proposed open's EFFECTIVE leverage as a decimal. Resolves the explicit `context.leverage` (Decimal-as-String) OR, when SizeSpec=leverage_implied, the `size.leverage` arm — Cedar cannot read either numerically (both are raw Strings). Servable from the action alone (no wallet read needed): leverage is in the action's own fields / live_inputs. Drives PERP-01.

action.description: The proposed open; explicit leverage or the leverage_implied SizeSpec arm yields the effective multiplier.

perp.funding_adverse_rate.name: perp.funding_adverse_rate
perp.funding_adverse_rate.description: PERP-04: funding rate SIGN-ALIGNED to the order side, returned as the adverse magnitude (decimal; >0 means funding works against the entry). The raw `fundingRate` is a Decimal-as-String and only means 'adverse' once folded against `side` (a long pays positive funding, a short receives it) — Cedar can neither sign-flip nor compare the String. Servable from the action's own fields/live_inputs (side + fundingRate). Drives PERP-04.

action.description: The proposed open; side selects the adverse funding sign, fundingRate the magnitude.

perp.margin_mode_transition.name: perp.margin_mode_transition
perp.margin_mode_transition.description: PERP-06: is this a risky isolated->cross margin switch for the market? The action carries only `newMode`; the CURRENT mode lives in wallet state (PerpPosition.margin_mode, State1), so detecting the direction requires reading state and comparing to newMode. Drives PERP-06.

market.description: Perp market whose current margin mode is read from state.

action.description: Carries newMode; compared against the state's current margin mode.

perp.crosses_zero.name: perp.crosses_zero
perp.crosses_zero.description: PERP-08: would this reduce/decrease order overshoot 0 and flip the position to the opposite side? Compares the order's decrease size to the CURRENT position size from wallet state (PerpPosition.size_base, State1), which is a U256-hex String not in the action context and not Cedar-comparable. Drives PERP-08.

action.description: The reduce/decrease order; its size is compared to the state position's size_base to detect a zero-crossing flip.

perp.cross_total_exposure_ratio.name: perp.cross_total_exposure_ratio
perp.cross_total_exposure_ratio.description: PERP-10: (sum of all held cross-margin positions' notional + the new order's notional) / account collateral. A whole-(perp)-account FOLD across positions: each held position's notional = size_base x its mark (State1 + Oracle), summed with the new notional, divided by collateral. Per-position notionals are U256-hex Strings — Cedar can neither sum nor divide them. Drives PERP-10.

action.description: The new open; its notional is added to the folded cross-position exposure, collateral is the denominator.

perp.leverage_increase.name: perp.leverage_increase
perp.leverage_increase.description: PERP-11: does this market have an OPEN position AND is the requested newLeverage higher than the current leverage? Existence (PerpPosition.size_base != 0) and current leverage (PerpPosition.leverage, decimal) are State1; `context.newLeverage` is a Decimal-as-String the action carries, so Cedar can't compare it to the state decimal. Drives PERP-11.

market.description: Perp market whose open position + current leverage are read from state.

action.description: Carries newLeverage; compared against the state position's current leverage.

perp.stop_trigger_misplaced.name: perp.stop_trigger_misplaced
perp.stop_trigger_misplaced.description: PERP-12: is the stop/take-profit triggerPrice on the wrong side of mark for its side + orderKind (e.g. a long's stop-loss above mark, fires instantly backwards)? triggerPrice and markPrice are both Decimal-as-String (Cedar can't order them) and the SL-vs-TP-vs-side rule is a multi-branch comparison. Servable from the action alone (triggerPrice/orderKind/side + live markPrice). Drives PERP-12.

action.description: The stop/TP order; triggerPrice vs (side, orderKind, live markPrice) consistency check.

claim.upfront_payment_required.name: claim.upfront_payment_required
claim.upfront_payment_required.description: AIR-06: does the claim bundle require an UPFRONT payment? True iff an erc20 approve OR a native (msg.value>0) transfer is ordered BEFORE the claim sub-action inside the same multicall bundle (advance-fee airdrop scam). A fold over the bundle's preceding sub-actions; the single lowered Claim context cannot see its siblings.
claim.upfront_payment_required.stateDependency: action structure only (multicall sibling sub-actions ordered before the claim); no wallet-state read

chain_id.description: CAIP-2 numeric chain id of the bundle.

action.description: The claim action plus its enclosing bundle context, so the fold can inspect sub-actions ordered before the claim.

claim.token_unrecognized.name: claim.token_unrecognized
claim.token_unrecognized.description: AIR-07: is the claimed token unrecognised on BOTH counts? True iff the token is NOT a key in WalletState.tokens (never held — durable state read) AND NOT present in an external token list. The AND reduces the false positives of held-history alone, which is weak because normal airdrops are first-time tokens.
stateDependency: external token list / registry (not the state DB)

owner.description: Evaluated wallet address — owner of the held-token set checked for membership.

token.description: The token to be received by the claim, normalised to a TokenKey for held-set membership and external-list lookup.

external.recipient_is_contract.name: external.recipient_is_contract
external.recipient_is_contract.description: AIR-08: does `address` have deployed bytecode (non-empty codehash via eth_getCode)? Distinguishes a contract recipient (possible drainer relay/proxy) from a plain EOA. A LIVE on-chain lookup that sits OUTSIDE the wallet snapshot — external info, not wallet state.
external.recipient_is_contract.stateDependency: live on-chain codehash (eth_getCode); not the state DB

chain_id.description: CAIP-2 numeric chain id the recipient lives on.

address.description: The claim recipient address whose codehash is checked.

launchpad.cumulative_committed_usd.name: launchpad.cumulative_committed_usd
launchpad.cumulative_committed_usd.description: LP-03: USD sum of every currently-held LaunchpadAllocation.paid across all open sales PLUS the in-flight Commit amount, valued at DB price. Cedar cannot fold a Vec<(TokenRef,U256)> across positions nor multiply by price, so this folds wallet state to one decimal. Drives LP-03 (live-holdings view; the time-window history sum over already-withdrawn/completed sales is GAP).

owner.description: Wallet whose open launchpad allocations are folded.

action.description: The in-flight Commit, whose amount is added to the existing allocation sum.

launchpad.curve_premium_bp.name: launchpad.curve_premium_bp
launchpad.curve_premium_bp.description: LP-04: price premium (in basis points) of a bonding-curve buy over fair value computed from the CURRENT curve reserves, versus the price the user is willing to pay (Commit amount / expectedTokenPrice). No durable wallet state; curve reserves are an external/oracle read and the bp premium is arithmetic Cedar cannot do. Drives LP-04.
launchpad.curve_premium_bp.stateDependency: bonding-curve reserves (external oracle / on-chain curve read; not wallet-state DB)

action.description: Commit action carrying amount / expectedTokenPrice (and platform/saleId to locate the curve).

launchpad.pay_token_match.name: launchpad.pay_token_match
launchpad.pay_token_match.description: LP-06: whether the Commit's pay token equals the token the sale declares it accepts. The accepted token is EXTERNAL (registry / Aggregate) and the pay-token identity lives at context.payToken.key.address (optional nested hex String, not comparable in Cedar), so the comparison is resolved host-side into one Bool. Drives LP-06.
launchpad.pay_token_match.stateDependency: sale-declared pay token (registry / Aggregate; not wallet-state DB)

action.description: Commit action carrying payToken + platform + saleId, used to resolve and compare the sale's accepted token.

stake.unlock_eta.name: stake.unlock_eta
stake.unlock_eta.description: STK-02: for an unstake/withdrawal request (decoded as Core::Unknown — no dedicated staking domain), match the target to the wallet's held StakeReceipt and report whether a withdrawal lock applies and the seconds until the funds become withdrawable. Reads TokenKind::StakeReceipt.unlock (Cliff{unlock_at}|Linear{start,end}|Cooldown{cooldown_secs}) plus the receipt's protocol. Lido 1-5d, EigenLayer +7d escrow.

chain_id.description: EVM chain id of the unstake target.

owner.description: Wallet whose StakeReceipt set is matched against the unstake target.

action.description: The unstake/withdrawal action (Core::Unknown target/calldata) used to locate the held StakeReceipt being exited.

valuation.gas_cost_ratio.name: valuation.gas_cost_ratio
valuation.gas_cost_ratio.description: GEN-08: ratio of the transaction's gas cost in USD to the USD value the action moves (gas_usd / action_value_usd). Cedar cannot divide two decimals, so the division is done server-side. gas_usd = gas_estimate × native price_value; action_value_usd = value_amount × value_asset price_value.

gas_estimate.description: Estimated gas cost (U256 hex / wei-form string) — numerator's gas-units basis.

value_asset.description: Asset whose USD value forms the ratio's denominator.

value_amount.description: Amount of value_asset moved (U256 hex / wei-form string).

token.outflow_pct_of_holding.name: token.outflow_pct_of_holding
token.outflow_pct_of_holding.description: GEN-06: the proposed action's amount as a percentage of the wallet's CURRENT holding of that exact token (amount / token_holdings.balance_amount × 100). Single-asset fraction-of-balance — distinct from the whole-portfolio fold portfolio.group_pct (whose denominator is total portfolio USD).

amount.description: Proposed outflow amount (U256 hex / wei-form string) — the percentage numerator.

permit.allowance_horizon.name: permit.allowance_horizon
permit.allowance_horizon.description: GEN-03: USD valuation of an off-chain permit's allowance and the validity horizon of its deadline, from one call. allowanceUsd = amount × token price_value (state). expiryHorizonSec = deadline − host now (the deadline is a Long action field but the horizon needs the host clock, which Cedar lacks). Emits TWO context fields (record).
stateDependency: host wall clock (for the deadline horizon, not wallet state)

amount.description: Permit allowance amount (U256 hex / wei-form string) — priced for allowanceUsd.

deadline.description: Signature expiry (unix seconds); horizon = deadline − now.

external.spender_is_eoa.name: external.spender_is_eoa
external.spender_is_eoa.description: GEN-14: is the approve target a plain wallet (EOA) rather than a contract? is_eoa = codehash(address) == empty, via a live eth_getCode view. External/on-chain view — NOT wallet state (codehash is not a durable refrigerated field).
external.spender_is_eoa.stateDependency: external on-chain view (eth_getCode); not the wallet-state DB

address.description: Spender address whose code (or absence thereof) decides EOA-ness.

approval.resulting_allowance_state.name: approval.resulting_allowance_state
approval.resulting_allowance_state.description: GEN-15: fold the existing on-chain allowance (State1, approvals_erc20.amount) with this increaseAllowance/approve added amount, then score the U256 sum (not Cedar-comparable). Emits the post-increase over-balance ratio (resulting allowance / available balance) and the unlimited flag. Two context fields from one call.

added_amount.description: The increaseAllowance/approve amount (U256 hex) folded onto the current allowance.

signature.chain_mismatch.name: signature.chain_mismatch
signature.chain_mismatch.description: GEN-17: does the EIP-712 signature's domain chainId differ from the chain the wallet is connected to? mismatch = domain_chain_id != EvalContext.chain. The action carries domain.chainId (Long) but the connected chain is the eval context, not an action field, so the comparison is made host-side.
signature.chain_mismatch.stateDependency: none (compares the signature domain chainId against EvalContext.chain; no wallet-state DB read)

chain_id.description: The chain currently connected / under evaluation (EvalContext.chain).

domain_chain_id.description: chainId carried by the signature's EIP-712 domain.

approval.already_granted.name: approval.already_granted
approval.already_granted.description: GEN-20: does a non-zero ERC20 allowance for (token, spender) already exist in wallet state (approvals_erc20.amount > 0)? Flags redundant / surprise re-approvals. ERC20-flavored sibling of approval.set_for_all_state (which covers the NFT operator case).

external.swap_floor_vs_fair_bps.name: external.swap_floor_vs_fair_bps
external.swap_floor_vs_fair_bps.description: UNI-04: divergence (basis points) between an AMM swap's contract floor exchange rate (direction.minAmountOut / amountIn) and the oracle fair price. The floor is a raw U256 HEX String on the swap context that Cedar cannot compare numerically, and the fair price is external, so both are reconciled here. Larger bp = the on-chain floor is worse than fair (hidden harsh slippage).
external.swap_floor_vs_fair_bps.stateDependency: external oracle mid-market price + decoded swap floor (not state DB)

action.description: The swap action whose exact-input floor (minAmountOut/amountIn) is compared to oracle fair value.

token.swap_out_classification.name: token.swap_out_classification
token.swap_out_classification.description: UNI-05: classification of the swap's tokenOut. `unclassified` = the token is held but its TokenKind is Unknown, OR it is unheld and the external registry cannot classify it; `honeypot` = an external sim/detector flags it buyable-but-not-sellable. Folds the wallet-state held-token kind read with an external probe and emits both flags from one call (record).

owner.description: Wallet whose held-token classification is read for the case tokenOut is already held.

asset.description: The token being bought, whose normality / honeypot status is judged.

external.swap_route_min_depth_usd.name: external.swap_route_min_depth_usd
external.swap_route_min_depth_usd.description: UNI-06: minimum per-hop pool depth (USD) across the swap's execution route, plus a shallow-hop flag. Only the summed routeEstimatedOut is exposed at the policy layer; the per-hop PoolState reserves (paths x hops) are reducer-internal and too complex for Cedar, so this folds them natively and surfaces the route's thinnest pool (record).
external.swap_route_min_depth_usd.stateDependency: live route PoolState reserves (SwapLiveInputs.route) + oracle USD pricing (not state DB)

action.description: The swap action whose live_inputs route is folded for per-hop pool depth.

external.swap_max_effective_fee_bp.name: external.swap_max_effective_fee_bp
external.swap_max_effective_fee_bp.description: UNI-08: maximum effective fee (basis points) observed across the swap route's hops. V4 dynamic-fee pools let a hook set the fee per swap and the current schema has no PoolKey dynamic-fee flag field, so the effective fee is taken from the reducer route math rather than a static field.
external.swap_max_effective_fee_bp.stateDependency: live route effective-fee per hop (SwapLiveInputs.route) (not state DB)

action.description: The swap action whose route hops are inspected for the max effective fee.

external.permit2_spender_is_router.name: external.permit2_spender_is_router
external.permit2_spender_is_router.description: UNI-09: does the Permit2 approval's spender equal the router that actually executes this swap (route aggregator.router / venue router), cross-checked against a known-router allowlist? Permit2 spender and the executing router are not both on the swap context and the known-router set is external, so this reconciles them. false = phishing signal.
external.permit2_spender_is_router.stateDependency: decoded Permit2 spender + route aggregator.router/venue + external known-router allowlist (not state DB)

action.description: The swap action whose bundled Permit2 spender is matched against its executing router.

external.swap_maxin_vs_fair_bps.name: external.swap_maxin_vs_fair_bps
external.swap_maxin_vs_fair_bps.description: UNI-10: overpay (basis points) of an exact-output swap's max payment ceiling versus fair value: direction.maxAmountIn compared to direction.amountOut converted at the oracle fair price. Both amounts are raw U256 HEX Strings on the swap context that Cedar cannot compare numerically, and the fair price is external. Larger bp = the ceiling allows paying well above fair.
external.swap_maxin_vs_fair_bps.stateDependency: external oracle mid-market price + decoded exact-output amounts (not state DB)

action.description: The exact-output swap action whose maxAmountIn ceiling is compared to fair value for amountOut.

intent.validity_horizon_sec.name: intent.validity_horizon_sec
intent.validity_horizon_sec.description: UNI-11: seconds from the evaluation clock (now) to a signed intent order's expiry (validUntil - now). validUntil is a Long on the context but the horizon needs the server clock, which Cedar lacks. A long horizon makes a signed open order a free option for the filler/solver.

intent.fill_vs_floor.name: intent.fill_vs_floor
intent.fill_vs_floor.description: UNI-12: shortfall (basis points) of a Dutch order's guaranteed floor (buyMin) below the expected fill (expectedFillPrice), evaluated only for orderKind == dutch. buyMin/expectedFillPrice are HEX/decimal Strings on the context that Cedar cannot compute a ratio over. Note: the current SignIntentOrder schema lacks start/end amount and decay-time fields, so the decay curve itself is unobservable; this measures the floor-vs-expected gap.
intent.fill_vs_floor.stateDependency: external oracle/expected-fill price + decoded order floor (not state DB)

action.description: The sign_intent_order action whose Dutch buyMin floor is compared to the expected fill.

token.swap_special_token.name: token.swap_special_token
token.swap_special_token.description: UNI-13: is the swap's tokenIn a special-transfer token? `special` = held token whose TokenKind is Wrapped with PegKind::Rebasing (durable state); `feeOnTransfer` = an external token-tax registry flags it as fee-on-transfer. Folds the held-state peg read with the external FoT probe and emits both flags from one call (record). Such tokens break unsupporting V3/V4 routers.

owner.description: Wallet whose held-token peg kind is read.

asset.description: The token being sold, checked for rebasing / fee-on-transfer behaviour.

lp.range_out_of_position.name: lp.range_out_of_position
lp.range_out_of_position.description: UNI-14: does a concentrated AddLiquidity position's tick range [params.range.tickLower, tickUpper] fail to contain the current price? The range bounds are Longs and currentPrice is a decimal String; converting a tick to a comparable price is beyond Cedar, so this does the tick<->price check natively. true = a one-sided range order (100% of one asset) with outsized IL exposure.

action.description: The add_liquidity action whose concentrated range is tested against the current price.

lp.exit_asymmetry.name: lp.exit_asymmetry
lp.exit_asymmetry.description: AMMLP-2: does removing this liquidity lock in impermanent loss because the held concentrated position has drifted heavily to one side of its entry range? Folds the held position's entry range (durable state) against the current price. true = exit-at-a-loss (distinct from UNI-14's one-sided entry). sv = PART: the remove path does not yet carry a current_price LiveField.

owner.description: Wallet whose held position entry range is read.

action.description: The remove_liquidity action identifying the position (nftKey / lpToken) being burned.

intent.pending_cap_over_balance.name: intent.pending_cap_over_balance
intent.pending_cap_over_balance.description: AMMLP-3: does the sum of active off-chain intent-order PermitCaps on the sell token, PLUS the new order's sell amount, exceed the wallet's held balance of that token? A reducer State2 fold over the OffchainLimitOrder pending set (PendingKind::OffchainLimitOrder + AssetCommitment::PermitCap) against token_holdings.balance. true = double-spend / all-fill-at-once exposure.

owner.description: Wallet whose pending-order caps and balance are folded.

action.description: The new sign_intent_order action whose sell amount is added to the active-cap sum.

intent.near_duplicate_pending.name: intent.near_duplicate_pending
intent.near_duplicate_pending.description: AMMLP-4: is the new intent order a near-duplicate of an already-open one (same venue, sell, buy, similar cap) in the active OffchainLimitOrder pending set? A reducer State2 membership check. true = likely accidental re-sign (double size / malicious double-fill).

owner.description: Wallet whose pending-order set is searched for a near-duplicate.

action.description: The new sign_intent_order action matched against the active order set.

intent.cancel_target_missing.name: intent.cancel_target_missing
intent.cancel_target_missing.description: AMMLP-5: is the cancel's orderHash absent from the wallet's active (Active/PartiallyFilled) intent-order set? A reducer State2 membership check. true = cancelling an already-filled/expired/cancelled order — wasted signature or a phishing 'looks-like-cancel' prompt.

owner.description: Wallet whose active order set is checked for the cancel target.

intent.isolated_fill_risk.name: intent.isolated_fill_risk
intent.isolated_fill_risk.description: AMMLP-6: combines solver-competition depth (context.competingOrders) with the market gap of the order's buyMin to flag a loose floor that can fill cheap to a single solver. true only when competition is thin AND the floor is loose versus market (keeps false positives down when competition is sufficient). The buyMin<<market half needs an oracle, so the two signals are joined here.
intent.isolated_fill_risk.stateDependency: live competing-orders count (SignIntentOrderLiveInputs.competing_orders) + oracle market price (not state DB)

action.description: The sign_intent_order action whose competition depth and buyMin-vs-market are jointly judged.

bridge.dest_chain_supported.name: bridge.dest_chain_supported
bridge.dest_chain_supported.description: BRG-04: is the destination chain of a bridge call (the dstChainId — LayerZero EID / CCTP domain / etc. — encoded inside the Unknown calldata) a member of the wallet's tracked chain set (WalletId.chains)? Extracts dstChainId from the calldata then tests set membership. Emits a single Bool so the warn fires when the destination chain is NOT one the wallet uses.

chain_id.description: Source EIP-155 chain ID of the bridge transaction.

target.description: Bridge contract address (Core::Unknown target), used to pick the right calldata layout.

calldata.description: Raw 0x-hex bridge calldata carrying the destination chain id.

bridge.min_out_haircut_bp.name: bridge.min_out_haircut_bp
bridge.min_out_haircut_bp.description: BRG-05: how harsh is the minimum-received floor (minAmountLD / amountOutMin) that the UI baked into a liquidity-bridge calldata, relative to a fair quote? Extracts the min-out from the Unknown calldata, prices the send against the refrigerated token price (token_holdings.price_value), and returns the haircut (fair_quote - min_out)/fair_quote in basis points (Long). Drives a warn when the floor is too low.

chain_id.description: Source EIP-155 chain ID of the bridge transaction.

target.description: Bridge contract address (Core::Unknown target), used to pick the right calldata layout.

calldata.description: Raw 0x-hex bridge calldata carrying the send amount and the min-received floor.

bridge.relayer_fee_bp.name: bridge.relayer_fee_bp
bridge.relayer_fee_bp.description: BRG-09: on an intent bridge (Across) the received amount is governed by the relayer fee = input - output spread, not slippage. Decodes inputAmount/outputAmount from the Unknown deposit calldata and returns the relayer fee in basis points (Long) so a warn fires when the fee exceeds the usual band. Also surfaces the variant where UI-shown fee and calldata outputAmount disagree.
bridge.relayer_fee_bp.stateDependency: Unknown deposit calldata (inputAmount/outputAmount) compared to a learned relayer-fee band; no wallet-state DB read

chain_id.description: Source EIP-155 chain ID of the bridge transaction.

target.description: Intent-bridge contract address (Core::Unknown target), used to pick the right deposit calldata layout.

calldata.description: Raw 0x-hex deposit calldata carrying inputAmount and outputAmount.

bridge.permission_change.name: bridge.permission_change
bridge.permission_change.description: BRG-10: does a zero-value cross-chain messaging call (LayerZero OApp setDelegate / setConfig / setPeer) reassign message-verification authority or trusted peer to an address that is NOT on the allowlist? Classifies the selector inside the Unknown calldata as a permission/config change and extracts the new delegate/peer, returning a single Bool that is true when it is a config-change message AND the delegate/peer is non-allowlisted.
bridge.permission_change.stateDependency: messaging-selector classification + delegate/peer allowlist (not a wallet-state DB read)

chain_id.description: Chain ID of the messaging transaction.

calldata.description: Raw 0x-hex calldata whose selector and arguments encode the permission/config change.

bridge.cctp_recipient_unreceivable.name: bridge.cctp_recipient_unreceivable
bridge.cctp_recipient_unreceivable.description: BRG-11: would a CCTP depositForBurn lock funds because the mintRecipient cannot receive at the destination? Decodes mintRecipient (bytes32), destinationDomain, and destinationCaller from the Unknown burn calldata and resolves whether the destination recipient is receivable (EOA / CCTP-aware), returning a single Bool that is true when the recipient is unreceivable.
bridge.cctp_recipient_unreceivable.stateDependency: CCTP burn-calldata decode + destination-chain recipient receivability lookup (not a wallet-state DB read)

chain_id.description: Source EIP-155 chain ID of the depositForBurn.

calldata.description: Raw 0x-hex depositForBurn calldata carrying mintRecipient, destinationDomain and destinationCaller.

bridge.refund_self.name: bridge.refund_self
bridge.refund_self.description: BRG-12: is the bridge refund/recovery address (a separate calldata argument, distinct from the recipient) NOT the wallet's own address? Introspects the Unknown calldata for the refund/recovery address and compares it to the wallet owner, returning a single Bool that is true when the refund address is not the wallet (so a failed transfer would be unrecoverable).

chain_id.description: Chain ID of the bridge transaction.

owner.description: Wallet owner address — the expected refund destination.

target.description: Bridge contract address (Core::Unknown target), used to pick the right calldata layout.

calldata.description: Raw 0x-hex bridge calldata carrying the refund/recovery address argument.

external.nft_floor.name: external.nft_floor
external.nft_floor.description: NFT-01/02/06: decode a marketplace order signature (Seaport/Blur, arriving as ActionBody::Unknown OffchainSig calldata) and value its consideration/offer against the collection floor. Emits the order-vs-floor ratio (Σconsideration ÷ floor, or listing-price ÷ floor, or incoming-offer ÷ floor) plus whether the listed NFT is one the wallet actually owns. Cedar cannot divide and the order body is undecoded, so this fuses order-decode + an external floor feed + a durable token_holdings ownership read into one call.
stateDependency: token_holdings (Balance::Owned for the listed ERC721/ERC1155 key)

chain_id.description: EIP-155 chain ID the collection lives on.

owner.description: Wallet address whose owned NFTs are checked against the order's offer items.

calldata.description: Raw 0x-hex order/signature payload (Core::Unknown calldata) the method decodes to extract offer/consideration items.

target.description: Marketplace/verifying contract the order is destined for; disambiguates the decoder (Seaport vs Blur).

external.seaport_order_shape.name: external.seaport_order_shape
external.seaport_order_shape.description: TOKENNFT-3: decode a Seaport order signature (arriving as ActionBody::Unknown OffchainSig calldata) and introspect its structural risk: whether any offer/consideration item is criteria-based with identifierOrCriteria==0 (wildcard 'any token id in the collection'), and whether the order's zone is outside the trusted-zone allowlist. Neither is a base context field, so the order must be decoded and the zone matched against an internal allowlist.
external.seaport_order_shape.stateDependency: external (Seaport order decode + trusted-zone allowlist; no wallet-state DB read)

chain_id.description: EIP-155 chain ID the order targets.

calldata.description: Raw 0x-hex Seaport order/signature payload (Core::Unknown calldata) the method decodes.

target.description: Seaport contract the order is destined for; selects the decoder version.

governance.voting_power_at_risk.name: governance.voting_power_at_risk
governance.voting_power_at_risk.description: GOV-02: how much governance voting power this delegate(address) call would re-point, expressed as a comparable decimal magnitude. Reads the durable StakeReceipt.voting_power for the governance `token` in wallet state (raw U256), scaling it to a decimal; when no durable receipt exists it falls back to the action's live `votingPower` live_input. Drives the size half of the re-delegation warn (the re-delegation half — delegatee != currentDelegate — is tested directly in Cedar over the action String fields).

token.description: Governance token whose voting power is being delegated.

action.description: Delegate action: carries delegatee, currentDelegate (live), and the U256 votingPower live_input used as the state-absent fallback.

curve.imbalance_skew.name: curve.imbalance_skew
curve.imbalance_skew.description: CURVE-1: how one-sided a Curve stableswap add_liquidity is versus the pool's current composition, as a decimal skew (deposit per-coin ratio vs pool per-coin balance ratio). The pool balances live in the reducer-internal live_inputs.pool_state (PoolState::StableV1 { balances } / Cryptoswap { balances }) which is NOT surfaced on AddLiquidityContext, and the deposit amounts in params.tokens are U256 hex strings Cedar cannot compare numerically — so the skew must be folded over pool balances + deposit amounts rather than read as an action-field. Drives the silent imbalance-fee warning.

action.description: The proposed Curve add_liquidity; its per-coin deposit amounts are compared against the live pool balance ratio.

curve.one_coin_penalty.name: curve.one_coin_penalty
curve.one_coin_penalty.description: CURVE-2: the imbalance penalty of a remove_liquidity_one_coin withdrawal versus a proportional (balanced) exit, as a decimal fraction (output shortfall vs balanced). Needs the pool's current per-coin balances (reducer-internal live_inputs.pool_state PoolState::StableV1.balances, not on RemoveLiquidityContext) against the burned LP amount; the withdrawal sizes in params.lpAmount/params.minOut are U256 hex strings Cedar cannot compare numerically. Folded over pool balances + burn amount, not readable as an action-field.

action.description: The proposed Curve remove_liquidity (pooled_burn one-coin variant); its burn amount and target coin are folded against live pool balances to quantify the penalty.

curve.pool_depeg_state.name: curve.pool_depeg_state
curve.pool_depeg_state.description: CURVE-3: live depeg state of a Curve peg pool, emitting TWO context fields from one call. pegDeviationBp = basis-point peg deviation BETWEEN the pool's two pegged assets (a pair deviation, distinct from the single-token token.peg_ratio); compositionOutOfBand = whether the pool's CURRENT per-coin composition has lurched outside its normal band (e.g. 95% stETH / 5% ETH). Both derive from the reducer-internal live_inputs.pool_state (StableV1.balances) plus the assets' declared peg refs; neither is a comparable action-field. Encodes the explorer's fragile-band notion (normally-safe pool dangerous only at a moment).

action.description: The proposed add/swap on a Curve peg pool; identifies the pool whose two pegged assets and live composition are evaluated.

curve.base_pool_depeg.name: curve.base_pool_depeg
curve.base_pool_depeg.description: CURVE-5: whether a Curve metapool's underlying base pool (3CRV / crvUSD, …) is depegging — true if ANY base-pool asset (e.g. USDT/USDC/DAI inside 3CRV) is outside its peg band. A recursive, one-level-down check distinct from CURVE-3 (the two visible pool assets): it decodes the target metapool to its base pool, then folds each base asset's peg ref against its live price. The base-pool composition is partial state (sv = PART; pool metadata is part registry feed) and the per-asset peg compare cannot be expressed as a Cedar action-field.

action.description: The proposed metapool add_liquidity; the metapool is decoded to its base pool, whose assets' pegs are folded.

token.risk_class.name: token.risk_class
token.risk_class.description: D1: classifies an acquired token's risk class (e.g. "Leveraged" for 2x/3x on-chain leveraged tokens like ETH2x), and the % of total portfolio USD already held in that same risk class AFTER the buy (whole-wallet fold). Leverage baked into the asset never appears in swap action params, so an external risk-grade classifier is required (TokenKind has no Leveraged variant). PERP-01's action-leverage cap is a separate control. Emits TWO context fields from one call.
stateDependency: external risk-class label per token (not in state DB)

owner.description: Wallet whose portfolio share of the leveraged class is measured.

asset.description: The token being acquired, whose risk class is classified.

order.swap_count_24h.name: order.swap_count_24h
order.swap_count_24h.description: F2: number of swaps the wallet executed in the trailing 24h window (over-trading / churn signal). Cross-request swap history over a sliding window, served by the bundled stat-window host (the same source already exposing swapCount24h); the turnover-ratio variant (volume / portfolio total) is a separate whole-wallet fold not covered here.
order.swap_count_24h.stateDependency: cross-request swap history over a 24h window ([H]); not in the state DB today

owner.description: Wallet whose 24h swap count is counted.

external.token_recent_return_pct.name: external.token_recent_return_pct
external.token_recent_return_pct.description: F4: a token's recent price return over a trailing window (default 5 days), as a percentage, from an oracle/momentum price feed. Signals a sharp run-up (FOMO top-chasing). The full F4 cooldown-timer enforcement needs the #7 history surface that does not exist yet; this method supplies the momentum signal half, which renders as a warn verdict.
external.token_recent_return_pct.stateDependency: external price-momentum feed (trailing-window return); not in the state DB

asset.description: The token being bought, whose recent return is measured.

window_days.description: Trailing window length in days for the return calculation (default 5).

token.interest_bearing.name: token.interest_bearing
token.interest_bearing.description: H2: is the acquired token interest/yield-bearing (riba) per its TokenKind? YieldReceipt / DebtReceipt (RateMode) / StakeReceipt variants in the state model structurally identify interest-bearing assets, so this is a DIRECT state read of the token's kind — no external feed needed. The content-screening half of H2 (gambling/adult) needs a separate external classifier and is out of scope for this method.

asset.description: The token being acquired, whose TokenKind is inspected for interest-bearing variants.

permit.full_balance_unknown_spender.name: permit.full_balance_unknown_spender
permit.full_balance_unknown_spender.stateDependency: Reads wallet state: (1) token_holdings balance for the owner+token to form the full-balance ratio denominator; (2) interaction/approval history (and/or user allowlist) for the owner to decide spender familiarity. No new state surface required — both reads exist conceptually in the simulation-state holdings + approval history; this method composes them. If approval/interaction history is not yet materialized in sim-server, spenderIsKnown degrades to allowlist-only until that read lands (call stays optional → fail-open).

params.token: TokenRef — token whose permit is being signed (from $.action.token)
params.amount: String — raw U256 hex permit allowance amount (from $.action.amount)

returns.fullBalancePct: decimal — permit `amount` / wallet's current balance of `token` × 100, 4dp dotted. Saturates at a large value when amount is U256::MAX / unlimited so the >90 branch fires. Mirrors precedent token.outflow_pct_of_holding.
returns.spenderIsKnown: bool — true iff `spender` is an address the wallet has previously approved/interacted with or that sits on the user's known-good/allowlist; false for first-seen / unfamiliar spenders.

order.recipient_lookalike_history.name: order.recipient_lookalike_history
order.recipient_lookalike_history.stateDependency: Reads the wallet's prior-recipient set (send history) maintained by the Aggregator/sim-server. The look-alike comparison (configurable prefix/suffix length, e.g. first 4 + last 4 hex chars match, middle mismatch) is performed entirely server-side; only a scalar Bool is projected into Cedar context. No send-history state is introduced into the stateless policy-engine layer — consistent with order.swap_count_24h and intent.near_duplicate_pending. Distinct from external.address_blocklisted: the comparison set is the wallet's OWN private history, not a public registry, hence needs-method (sim-server) rather than needs-external.

returns.lookalike: Bool — true iff `recipient` matches a prior recipient on prefix AND suffix but differs in the middle, AND `recipient` itself has never been a recipient of `owner` before (a seeded doppelgänger). false otherwise (including when send-history is empty).

external.recipient_first_seen.name: external.recipient_first_seen
external.recipient_first_seen.description: Returns whether `from` has ever previously sent funds to / interacted with `recipient` on `chain_id`. Host reads the wallet's on-chain counterparty history (explorer / tx index) outside the stateless
external.recipient_first_seen.stateDependency: external-history (wallet on-chain counterparty history). No new in-engine Cedar state surface; engine stays stateless and reads only the Bool. Mirrors external.address_blocklisted host-lookup pattern.

params.chain_id: String — CAIP-2 / numeric chain id of the transfer (from $.root.chain_id)

returns.first_time: Bool — true iff `from` has no prior outbound transfer/interaction to `recipient` on `chain_id`. Projected into context.custom.firstTimeRecipient.

valuation.native_reserve_after.name: valuation.native_reserve_after
valuation.native_reserve_after.description: (policy-factory adopted) swap-native-gas-starvation-warn: would this action leave native gas balance below a reserve floor? Reads WalletState native holding + gas/spend; State2/reducer family (cf. position.health_factor_after).
valuation.native_reserve_after.stateDependency: Reads ONLY the real WalletState native gas holding (TokenHolding.kind==NativeGas; TokenHolding.balance U256) plus action gas_estimate and conditional native spend_amount. Host does the U256 subtract+floor compare; Cedar reads one projected Bool. No new Cedar state surface. optional => fail-open until sim-server serves it.

gas_estimate.description: U256 hex gas cost of this action

spend_asset.description: token being sold; decides if spend debits native

external.spender_reputation.name: external.spender_reputation
external.spender_reputation.description: (policy-factory adopted) approve-spender-unknown-contract-warn: spender HAS code (contract via eth_getCode) AND not in a known-good/allowlist registry. Complement of external.spender_is_eoa (which fires on EOA spenders).
external.spender_reputation.stateDependency: external reputation/allowlist feed + eth_getCode codehash probe; NOT wallet-state DB. Cedar reads only the Bool. optional => fail-open.

external.token_symbol_canonical_mismatch.name: external.token_symbol_canonical_mismatch
external.token_symbol_canonical_mismatch.description: (policy-factory adopted) swap-out-token-symbol-spoof-warn: tokenOut's symbol resolves to a known canonical coin whose official contract != tokenOut's address (ticker impersonation at a non-official contract). Inverse of token.swap_out_classification's 'unclassified'.
external.token_symbol_canonical_mismatch.stateDependency: external symbol->canonical-address registry (curated well-known tickers->official contract per chain); external reference data, NOT per-wallet state. optional => fail-open.

asset.description: token being bought; symbol resolved host-side from address

lp.input_rebasing.name: lp.input_rebasing
lp.input_rebasing.description: Does ANY token supplied in this add_liquidity carry TokenKind::Wrapped{peg_kind: Rebasing} (e.g. Lido stETH)? Rebasing LSTs supplied to a rebase-unaware AMM stop accruing rewards to the depositor. Drives lido-rebasing-steth-as-lp-warn.

owner.description: Wallet supplying liquidity; used to read held token kinds.

action.description: The add_liquidity action whose pooled tokens are inspected for a rebasing LST.
